- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Notes

See [design.md](design.md)


## jj tips

For users new to jj see [jj-tips.md](jj-tips.md).

```
## Chores format

Filename: "chores-XX.md"
example: chores-01.md

Format of section labels: "## <short description> (X.Y.Z)"
example: "## Topic format description (0.1.0)"

Example chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Do something (1.3.1)

Describe something
```

## Versioning during development

This is using jujustiu, jj + git and we'll see how it goes. Below is my
git workflow, jj will be different but we'll have to discover that as
we go.

Every plan must start with a version bump. Choose the approach based on scope:

- **Single-step** (recommended for mechanical/focused changes): bump directly to
  `X.Y.Z`, implement in one commit. Simpler history.
- **Multi-step** (for exploratory/large changes): bump to `X.Y.Z-devN`, implement
  across multiple commits, final commit removes `-devN`.

The plan should recommend one approach and get user approval before starting.

For multi-step:
1. Bump version to `X.Y.Z-devN` with a plan and commit as a chore marker
2. Implement in one or more `-devN` commits (bump N as needed)
3. Final commit removes `-devN`, updates todo/chores — this is the "done" marker

The final release commit (without `-devN`) signals completion rather than amending
prior commits. This keeps the git history readable and makes it easy to see which
commits were exploratory vs final.

## Todo format

Todo.md contains two main sections "Todo" and "Done" each item is a
short explanations of a tasks and links to more details using 1 or more
references.

Multiple references must be separated: `[2],[3]` not `[2,3]` or `[2][3]`.
In markdown, `[2,3]` is a single ref key (won't resolve) and `[2][3]`
is parsed as display text `2` with ref key `3` (so `[2]` won't resolve).

Examples:

# Todo

- Add new feature X [details](chores-01.md#feature-x)
- Fix bug Y [1]

# Done

- Fixed issue Z [2],[3]

[1]: chores-01bugs.md#bug-y

# actor-x1

## Overview

Actor-x1 is a system that uses Communicating
Sequential Process (CSP) invented by Tony Hoare
In 1978 and is commonly termed the actor model.

 - There will be a management system (MS) that:

   - Provides the capability to interact with
     the runtime providing control and to capture
     logs and assists in developing the app
   - Creates an hello world scaffolding
   - provides discovery capabilities of other
     other actors.

 - A runtime that drives the system and provides
   resources and services for the system
   - interfaces between the manager and the application
 
 - One or more actors running on one or more
   threads. Each actor adheres to these
   invariants:
   - Has on basic entry point; handler()
   - The handler() handles one message at a time and
     must run to completion as quickly as possible.
   - The handler() never ever blocks
   - If a handler() has tasks that take to much time
     a technique that can be used is to spawn
     threads to do work. That thread would then use
     messages to communicate with its parent and
     other actors as it does its work.

## Future capabilities
   - I would like Actors to be implemented as an HSM.
     This potentially allows an Actor to implement
     sophisticated algorithms and prove correct operation.

## Stage1 runtime

Design and implement the simplest runtime possible

### Message

struct Message {}

### Actor

Actor implements an Actor trait

trait Actor {
  // Handles a message 
  fn handle_message(msg: Message)

  // other methods..
}

### Goal1

- Run two actors on one thread.
- A single message which is an empty struct
- Message ping pongs between the two actors
  for duration in seconds specified as a f64
  in seconds from the command line

## Goal2

- Same as Goal1 but each actor on a separate thread.

## Stage2 runtime

We will design and implement the slightly more capable runtime

It supports 3 actors A, B and C

### Message

struct Message {
  src_id: u32,
  dst_id: u32,
  send_count: u64,
}

### Actor

Actor implements an Actor trait

trait Actor {
  // Create a new actor
  fn new(id: u32, name: &str) -> Self;

  // Return the actors name
  fn name(&Self) -> &str;

  // Handle a message
  fn handle_message(msg: mut Message);

  // other methods..
}

### Goal1

- Run 3 actors on one thread.
- Runtime "sends" 5 messages each to a random destination actor
- Each actor forwards the message they receive to a random
  dst_id that they select.

### Goal2
- Same as Goal1 but each actor on a separate thread.

1	# Chores-01
2	
3	Discussions and notes on various chores in github compatible markdown.
4	There is also a [todo.md](todo.md) file and it tracks tasks and in
5	general there should be a chore section for each task with the why
6	and how this task will be completed.
7	
8	See [Chores format](README.md#chores-format)
9	
10	## Stage 1 runtime — plan marker (0.1.0-0)
11	
12	Begins the Stage 1 implementation described in [design.md](design.md).
13	Multi-step ladder (see [Versioning during development](../CLAUDE.md#versioning)):
14	
15	- `0.1.0-0` — bootstrap Cargo crate + this plan marker (no behavior).
16	- `0.1.0-1` — Goal1: two actors, single thread, ping-pong an empty
17	  `Message` for a caller-supplied duration in seconds. Reports
18	  total messages handled.
19	- `0.1.0-2` — vendor `tprobe2` (and its transitive deps: `band_table`,
20	  `ticks`, `fmt_commas*`) from `../iiac-perf/src` into `src/perf/`.
21	  Wrap per-dispatch `handle_message` calls with `probe.start`/`probe.end`
22	  using `site_id = actor_id`; print a band-table report at shutdown
23	  (ns by default, `-t/--ticks` for raw ticks). The bot thinks promoting
24	  this to a shared crate is worthwhile once Stage 2 is underway, but
25	  copying in now avoids a cross-repo dependency for the PoC.
26	- `0.1.0-3` — Goal2: same workload as Goal1 but each actor on its own
27	  thread, communicating via `std::sync::mpsc` channels. Shutdown by
28	  dropping senders. Probe instrumented the same way (per-thread
29	  `TProbe2`, report on join).
30	- `0.1.0` — final: drop the `-N` suffix, update `notes/todo.md` and
31	  `notes/README.md`.
32	
33	### Design decisions recorded here
34	
35	- **Actor trait signature** deviates from the design sketch by adding
36	  `&mut self` and a `&mut dyn Context` parameter, where `Context::send(dst_id, msg)`
37	  is the only way for a handler to emit messages. Required because
38	  the empty `Message {}` carries no reply-to information and the
39	  sketched signature `fn handle_message(msg: Message)` has nowhere
40	  to thread outbound sends through.
41	- **Two `Context` implementations**: the single-threaded runtime's
42	  context writes to a `VecDeque`; the multi-threaded runtime's
43	  context holds `Sender<Message>` per destination. Actors see
44	  only `&mut dyn Context` and are agnostic to which runtime is
45	  driving them.
46	- **x86_64-only for now**: `tprobe2`'s tick counter uses `rdtsc`
47	  and `iiac-perf/src/ticks.rs` emits a `compile_error!` on other
48	  arches. Development target is x86_64 so this is acceptable; the
49	  restriction is documented at the vendored module.
50	
51	## Goal1: single-thread ping-pong runtime (0.1.0-1)
52	
53	Implements Goal1 of Stage 1: two actors, one thread, ping-pong an
54	empty [`Message`] for a caller-supplied duration in seconds, then
55	report the message count and throughput.
56	
57	- `src/lib.rs`: `Message` unit struct, `Actor` trait
58	  (`handle_message(&mut self, &mut dyn Context, Message)`),
59	  `Context` trait (`send(dst_id: u32, msg: Message)`), and
60	  `pub mod runtime`.
61	- `src/runtime.rs`: `SingleThreadRuntime` owns a
62	  `Vec<Box<dyn Actor>>` and a `VecDeque<(u32, Message)>`; `run_for`
63	  pops a message, field-split-borrows `actors` and `queue`,
64	  constructs a private `SingleCtx` wrapping the queue, dispatches,
65	  counts. Terminates on deadline or queue drain. Unit tests cover
66	  seed drain, bounded ping-pong count (11 = seed + 5·2), and
67	  sequential id assignment.
68	- `src/bin/goal1.rs`: CLI takes one positional `<duration_secs>`
69	  f64; constructs two `PingPongActor`s (each replies once per
70	  received message to its peer), seeds one message into actor 0,
71	  runs, prints `goal1: <count> messages in <secs>s (<M msg/s>)`.
72	
73	Smoke run on this box: 0.5 s → ~19.3 M messages → ~38.7 M msg/s.
74	
75	### Deviation from the design sketch
76	
77	`struct Message {}` in `design.md` is realised as a unit struct
78	`pub struct Message;` — equivalent externally, instantiates as
79	`Message` rather than `Message {}`. Matches clippy's preference for
80	empty types; trivial to convert back if literal match matters.
81	
82	## Vendored perf probe + Goal1 instrumentation (0.1.0-2)
83	
84	Copies `tprobe2` and its transitive dependencies into the crate and
85	wraps every `handle_message` dispatch with a probe scope so Goal1
86	reports per-dispatch latency alongside message count.
87	
88	- `Cargo.toml`: version bump to 0.1.0-2; add `hdrhistogram = "7"` and
89	  `minstant = "0.1"` dependencies.
90	- `src/perf/mod.rs`: new module root; documents vendor source path
91	  (`../iiac-perf/src`) and divergences (import paths, `// OK: …`
92	  on `unwrap*`, one diagnostic string).
93	- `src/perf/tprobe2.rs`: vendored from `iiac-perf/src/tprobe2.rs`.
94	  Imports rewritten to `crate::perf::{band_table, ticks}`; two
95	  `unwrap` calls gained `// OK: …` justifications.
96	- `src/perf/band_table.rs`: vendored from `iiac-perf/src/band_table.rs`.
97	  Imports rewritten to `crate::perf::fmt` and `crate::perf::ticks`;
98	  `unwrap_or(0)` calls gained `// OK: …` justifications.
99	- `src/perf/ticks.rs`: vendored unchanged except for the
100	  `compile_error!` diagnostic now mentions `actor-x1 probes`.
101	- `src/perf/ticks/x86_64.rs`: vendored unchanged.
102	- `src/perf/fmt.rs`: extracted `fmt_commas` and `fmt_commas_f64`
103	  from `iiac-perf/src/harness.rs` (lines 160–188) — avoids vendoring
104	  the 393-line harness for two helpers. `unwrap_or(0)` gained a
105	  `// OK: …` justification.
106	- `src/lib.rs`: adds `pub mod perf;`.
107	- `src/runtime.rs`: `SingleThreadRuntime::new(probe_name)` now takes
108	  a name, embeds a `TProbe2`, and wraps each `handle_message` call
109	  with `probe.start(dst_id as u64)` / `probe.end(id)`. `Default` impl
110	  dropped (new takes an arg). `probe_mut()` exposes the probe for
111	  end-of-run reporting. Unit tests pass a probe name.
112	- `src/bin/goal1.rs`: new arg parser supports `-t` / `--ticks`
113	  (raw-ticks display); runtime constructed as `"goal1.dispatch"`;
114	  calls `rt.probe_mut().report(as_ticks)` after the dispatch loop.
115	  Usage errors now `exit(2)` with a usage line instead of panicking.
116	
117	### Smoke measurements (release build)
118	
119	- `goal1 0.5` — 10.56 M messages in 0.5s (~21.1 M msg/s).
120	  Per-dispatch: mean 9 ns, mean min-p99 9 ns, p99 max 67.5 µs
121	  (likely an OS interrupt in the tail).
122	- `goal1 0.5 -t` — 10.57 M messages in 0.5s (~21.1 M msg/s).
123	  Per-dispatch: mean 35 tk, mean min-p99 34 tk, p99 max 114,687 tk.
124	
125	Adding the probe cuts throughput roughly in half vs. 0.1.0-1
126	(~38.7 → ~21.1 M msg/s). The bot thinks the delta is dominated by
127	two `rdtsc` reads plus `Vec::push` per dispatch (~20 ns of the
128	~47 ns per-dispatch cost); the 9 ns measured by the probe is the
129	`handle_message` body alone.
130	
131	## Inner batching, warmup, clap (0.1.0-3)
132	
133	Adds CLI polish and measurement-quality improvements to Goal1.
134	Scope:
135	
136	- **Inner batching** — one probe scope now covers `--inner N`
137	  consecutive dispatches (default 1). At larger `N` the probe's
138	  two-`rdtsc`-plus-push apparatus cost is amortized, and stored
139	  tick values land in a range where the histogram's 0.1 %-relative
140	  buckets actually resolve jitter.
141	- **Warmup** — `--warmup SECS` (default **10.0**) runs the same
142	  dispatch loop with the probe active, then clears probe state;
143	  measurement phase inherits stable-state CPU freq / cache /
144	  branch-predictor conditions. Warmup message counts are never
145	  reported.
146	- **clap** — hand-rolled arg parsing replaced with a derive-based
147	  `Cli`. Validates non-negative finite `duration_s` and `warmup`;
148	  enforces `inner ≥ 1`. Auto-generated `--help` / `--version`.
149	- **TProbe2 additions** (vendored-code divergence from iiac-perf):
150	  - `Record` gains a `batch: u64` field.
151	  - `TProbe2::end_batch(id, n)` appends with `batch = n`; existing
152	    `end(id)` sets `batch = 1`.
153	  - `TProbe2::report` now divides stored deltas by `batch` at
154	    drain time so the histogram always holds per-event cost,
155	    regardless of scope granularity.
156	  - `TProbe2::clear()` resets records buffer and histogram;
157	    called between warmup and measurement.
158	
159	File-level edits:
160	
161	- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.1.0-3; add
162	  `clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive", "wrap_help"] }`.
163	- `src/perf/tprobe2.rs`: `Record.batch`, `end_batch`, `clear`,
164	  per-event division in `report`. Doc comment at file head lists
165	  divergences from upstream. Three new tests
166	  (`end_batch_stores_batch_size`, `report_divides_batched_delta`,
167	  `clear_empties_records_and_hist`).
168	- `src/runtime.rs`: `run_for(duration, inner)` wraps each batch
169	  of `inner` dispatches in a probe scope; site_id is the first
170	  `dst_id` in the batch. Partial trailing batches (queue empties
171	  mid-scope) are discarded — scope dropped, count not incremented
172	  — so throughput numbers reflect whole-batch work only. New test
173	  `partial_trailing_batch_is_dropped` covers the drop path.
174	- `src/bin/goal1.rs`: clap-derive `Cli` with `duration_s`,
175	  `--warmup`, `--inner`, `-t/--ticks`. Runs warmup, clears probe,
176	  runs measurement, prints count/throughput (now also reports
177	  `inner=N`), then calls `probe.report`.
178	
179	### Smoke measurements (release, `--warmup 0`)
180	
181	| Args | Throughput | Probe mean (min-p99) | Notes |
182	|---|---|---|---|
183	| `0.5` (inner=1) | 19.7 M msg/s | 9 ns | Apparatus overhead dominates, distribution stuck in two spike buckets |
184	| `0.5 --inner 1000` | 205 M msg/s | 5 ns | ~10× throughput; per-dispatch mean drops because apparatus is amortized |
185	| `0.5 --inner 1000 -t` | 204 M msg/s | 18 tk | Ticks view; tail p99-max 449 tk vs ~115 k tk at inner=1 (batch smooths OS interrupts) |
186	
187	Warmup wall-clock sanity: `goal1 0.3 --warmup 1 --inner 1000` ran
188	in 1.33s total. Argv error paths (`-1`, `--inner 0`, `--warmup nan`)
189	all exit 2 with clap-formatted messages.
190	
191	The bot thinks the inner=1 vs inner=1000 gap (9 ns vs 5 ns
192	per-event) is ~4 ns of apparatus overhead; 0.1.0-5's overhead
193	calibration will confirm that estimate directly.
194	
195	## Always render all 12 percentile bands (0.1.0-4)
196	
197	Previously the renderer skipped any band where no hdrhistogram
198	bucket was assigned by mid-rank (`if band_count[i] == 0 { continue;
199	}`), so tight distributions produced reports with only 3–5 visible
200	rows and it was hard to tell at a glance whether a band was
201	genuinely empty or just not rendered. This step always emits all
202	12 rows (`min-p1` through `p99-max`); empty bands render as zeros
203	in every numeric column.
204	
205	File-level edits:
206	
207	- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.1.0-4.
208	- `src/perf/band_table.rs`: drop the skip-on-empty guard; the band
209	  loop now computes `(first_v, last_v, range_v, mean_v)` from
210	  either the band aggregates (for non-empty bands) or `(0, 0, 0, 0)`
211	  for empty ones. `unwrap_or(0)` `// OK: …` justifications updated
212	  ("rows always contains n_bands=12 entries").
213	
214	### Display example (inner=10, 0.5 s)
215	
216	All 12 rows always visible — the zero-valued bands make the
217	distribution's shape explicit:
218	
219	```
220	    min-p1        4 ns        4 ns        0 ns        8,712     4 ns
221	    p1-p10        0 ns        0 ns        0 ns            0     0 ns
222	    ...
223	    p40-p50       5 ns        5 ns        0 ns    4,794,952     5 ns
224	    ...
225	    p99-max       7 ns    3,197 ns    3,190 ns       61,924    10 ns
226	```
227	
228	### Caveat — this only fixes visibility, not the bucket-into-band
229	mapping
230	
231	The renderer still walks hdrhistogram buckets and assigns each
232	bucket to exactly one band by cumulative mid-rank. In the inner=10
233	smoke above, 4,794,952 samples (~89 %) land in `p40-p50` while
234	`p1-p10` through `p30-p40` stay at zero — the single hot bucket at
235	"5 ns" got attributed to one band, not spread across the percentile
236	range its samples actually span. The bot thinks the principled
237	fix (switch to `hist.value_at_quantile(q)` per band boundary) is
238	worth considering if the zero-band display starts feeling
239	misleading; deferred for now.
240	
241	### Divergence from upstream
242	
243	Further modification of the vendored `src/perf/band_table.rs`.
244	Noted in the perf module's divergence list; already has other
245	actor-x1-specific edits (`// OK:` comments, import paths).
246	
247	## CLI polish: help width + version banner (0.1.0-5)
248	
249	Two small quality-of-life tweaks to the `goal1` CLI.
250	
251	### Help width cap at 80 cols
252	
253	clap's default help-width uses the terminal width, which on wide
254	terminals produces hard-to-scan long lines for the `--warmup` and
255	`--inner` descriptions. `max_term_width = 80` caps rendering at
256	80 columns while still using terminal width if smaller.
257	
258	### Version banner
259	
260	First line of `goal1` output — and of `goal1 -h` — is now
261	`{CARGO_PKG_NAME} {CARGO_PKG_VERSION}` (e.g. `actor-x1 0.1.0-5`),
262	matching what `--version` already prints. Helps identify which
263	build produced a given result sheet (or which help text a user is
264	reading) when lines are pasted out of context. The existing
265	`goal1: …` line below carries the binary identity, so the banner
266	doesn't repeat "goal1". Implemented in two places: a `println!`
267	at the top of `main` for normal runs, and clap's `before_help`
268	attribute for help output (`concat!(env!(CARGO_PKG_NAME), " ",
269	env!(CARGO_PKG_VERSION))` — compile-time concat of package env
270	vars).
271	
272	### File-level edits
273	
274	- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.1.0-5.
275	- `src/bin/goal1.rs`: extend the `Cli` struct's `#[command(...)]`
276	  attribute with `max_term_width = 80` and
277	  `before_help = concat!(env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"), " ", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"))`;
278	  `println!` the same banner at the top of `main` for
279	  non-help runs.
280	
281	## Apparatus overhead calibration (0.1.0-6)
282	
283	Adds a calibration phase between warmup and measurement that fits
284	the probe's framing cost (the bias on every stored
285	`end_tsc − start_tsc` delta) and subtracts the appropriate per-
286	event correction at report time. `--raw` opts out.
287	
288	### Why two-point (not single-point)
289	
290	`rdtsc` isn't a serializing instruction, so two back-to-back reads
291	with nothing between can execute out-of-order and measure ~0 ticks
292	— below the true apparatus cost. `_mm_lfence` bracketing drains
293	the pipeline harder than real work does (measured 38 tk on this
294	box, 2× the real bias). The two-point fit gives the right answer
295	by varying `inner` on an empty bench and solving
296	`raw_delta = framing + inner · loop_per_iter` in `f64` (no
297	integer-division loss at small values).
298	
299	On this box the fit lands on **framing = 21 tk (~5.5 ns)**,
300	reproducible across runs. With the correction applied,
301	`inner=1` probe mean drops from 9 ns (raw) to 4 ns, which matches
302	the `inner=1000` raw mean of 5 ns (where the correction is
303	negligible) — both phases converge on the same actual
304	`handle_message` cost.
305	
306	### File-level edits
307	
308	- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.1.0-6.
309	- `src/perf/overhead.rs`: new module. Constants `CAL_WARMUP = 10_000`,
310	  `CAL_SAMPLES = 1_000`, `N_LOW = 100`, `N_HIGH = 10_000`. `Overhead`
311	  struct carries `framing_ticks`, `loop_per_iter_ticks`,
312	  `cal_raw_low_ticks`, `cal_raw_high_ticks`, `cal_duration`.
313	  `calibrate()` runs the fit; `Overhead::per_event_ticks(batch)`
314	  returns `framing_ticks / batch.max(1)` for subtraction. Two unit
315	  tests (amortization math + calibration smoke).
316	- `src/perf/mod.rs`: `pub mod overhead` + re-export
317	  `Overhead`/`calibrate`.
318	- `src/perf/tprobe2.rs`: `TProbe2::report` signature gains
319	  `overhead: Option<&Overhead>`; drain loop subtracts
320	  `ovh.per_event_ticks(batch)` from each `per_event_raw` before
321	  histogram insertion (via `saturating_sub`, clamp at 1). New
322	  test `report_subtracts_overhead`. Old tests updated to pass `None`.
323	- `src/bin/goal1.rs`: new `--raw` flag; calibrates on the warmed
324	  system between `probe.clear()` and the measurement `run_for`;
325	  prints an `apparatus: …` diagnostic line before the band-table
326	  report.
327	
328	### What's *not* subtracted
329	
330	Only `framing_ticks`. `loop_per_iter_ticks` (from the black_box
331	empty bench) is kept on `Overhead` for diagnostic visibility but
332	deliberately not subtracted — the real dispatch loop's per-iter
333	cost (queue pop, deadline check, SingleCtx construction, dynamic
334	dispatch) is part of what the user wants to measure, and
335	`black_box(1)`'s loop_per_iter doesn't model it anyway. User
336	already signed off on this scope.
337	
338	### Smoke
339	
340	- `goal1 0.5 --warmup 0 --inner 1` — 19.9 M msg/s, apparatus
341	  21 tk / 5.54 ns, probe mean min-p99 **4 ns** (from 9 ns raw).
342	- `goal1 0.5 --warmup 0 --inner 1000` — 204 M msg/s, apparatus
343	  correction 0 tk (rounds down), probe mean min-p99 5 ns
344	  (unchanged — raw already clean at this batch).
345	- `goal1 0.5 --warmup 0 --inner 1 --raw` — apparatus: "raw (no
346	  overhead subtraction)"; no calibration run.
347	- 14/14 tests pass. Calibration adds ~10 ms to default runs.
348	
349	## Goal2: per-actor threads + mpsc channels (0.1.0-7)
350	
351	Implements Goal2 of Stage 1: two actors running on their own
352	threads, ping-ponging an empty [`Message`] over `std::sync::mpsc`
353	channels, with the same warmup/measurement/calibration lifecycle
354	as goal1.
355	
356	### Design decisions
357	
358	- **Factory closures instead of pre-boxed actors.** `add_actor`
359	  takes `FnOnce() -> A` where `A: Actor + Send + 'static`; the
360	  factory runs inside the actor's own thread. Keeps the [`Actor`]
361	  trait free of a `Send` bound and matches how real actor systems
362	  typically spawn state.
363	- **In-band shutdown via internal `Signal` enum.** Channel payload
364	  is `Signal { User(Message), ClearProbe, Shutdown }`, not
365	  `Message` directly. Main thread injects `ClearProbe` at the
366	  warmup → measurement boundary and `Shutdown` after measurement;
367	  actors match on the variant in their `recv()` loop. Avoids an
368	  out-of-band atomic flag and the associated polling.
369	- **`inner` fixed at 1.** Ping-pong keeps at most one message in
370	  flight per channel, so batched probe scopes (`inner > 1`) could
371	  never fill — every scope would hit the partial-batch drop path.
372	  The `--inner` flag from goal1 is not exposed; the hardcoded
373	  `inner=1` is reflected in the throughput line.
374	- **Per-actor probes, merged reporting.** Each actor thread owns
375	  a [`TProbe2`] named `"goal2.dispatch.actor{id}"`. Main collects
376	  `(count, probe)` on join and prints a per-actor band table (not
377	  merged — two independent distributions are more informative
378	  than one blended histogram in a ping-pong workload where the
379	  actors should be near-identical).
380	- **Calibration pre-spawn on main thread.** Matches goal1's
381	  calibration code path. Same CPU → same `Overhead` applies to
382	  all actor threads. Could be refined later to calibrate
383	  per-thread, but unnecessary for Stage 1.
384	
385	### File-level edits
386	
387	- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.1.0-7.
388	- `src/runtime.rs`: add `MultiThreadRuntime`, `Signal` enum,
389	  `ActorFactory` alias, `MultiCtx`, and `actor_loop` helper below
390	  the existing single-thread code. New `#[cfg(test)] mod mt_tests`
391	  with a ping-pong smoke test (50 ms warmup + 50 ms measurement).
392	- `src/bin/goal2.rs`: new binary mirroring goal1's CLI shape
393	  (version banner, help width cap, --warmup, -t/--ticks, --raw),
394	  no --inner. Constructs `MultiThreadRuntime`, adds two
395	  `PingPongActor` factories, seeds one message, calls
396	  `rt.run(warmup, measurement)`, prints summary + apparatus +
397	  per-actor reports.
398	
399	### Smoke (release, `--warmup 0`)
400	
401	- `goal2 0.5` — 116,495 messages in 0.5 s (~0.23 M msg/s).
402	  Mean per-dispatch ~1.17 µs; median ~1.34 µs; p99 tail to
403	  ~22 µs (mostly scheduling jitter). Actors near-symmetric
404	  (58,247 vs 58,248 messages).
405	- `goal2 -h` — wraps at 80 cols, shows the `actor-x1 0.1.0-7`
406	  banner, no `--inner` flag.
407	- `goal2 0.2 --raw` — apparatus: `raw (no overhead subtraction)`;
408	  calibration skipped.
409	- goal1 unchanged: still ~200 M msg/s at `--inner 1000`.
410	- Tests: 15/15 pass (adds `multi_thread_runs_measures_and_shuts_down_cleanly`).
411	
412	### The ~250× single-thread gap
413	
414	Goal1 at `inner=1000` hits ~205 M msg/s (~5 ns/dispatch);
415	goal2 at `inner=1` hits ~0.23 M msg/s (~4 µs/round-trip, ~1.2 µs
416	per-event inside the probe scope). The bot thinks the gap is
417	dominated by: (1) mpsc's `Mutex` + condvar wake for every
418	message, (2) two cross-thread context switches per ping-pong
419	round, (3) cache-line bouncing between the two actors' threads.
420	An unbounded channel or a lock-free mpsc implementation would
421	close some of this but the context-switch floor is fundamental.
422	
423	## `--pin` for thread CPU affinity (0.1.0-8)
424	
425	Adds `--pin <CORES>` to both binaries. Tightens the stdev of
426	probe reports by eliminating OS thread-migration noise, at the
427	cost of exposing placement sensitivity in goal2 (pinning to
428	cross-CCX cores raises the mean because every message forces a
429	cross-complex cache coherence round-trip).
430	
431	Adapted from `../iiac-perf/src/pin.rs` with only the two
432	primitives we need (`parse_cores`, `pin_current`); the affinity
433	save/restore, `--no-pin-cal`, and mask-summary helpers are
434	elided — Stage 1 doesn't need them.
435	
436	### File-level edits
437	
438	- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.1.0-8; add `core_affinity = "0.8"`.
439	- `src/perf/pin.rs`: new module. `parse_cores(&str) -> Result<Vec<usize>, String>`
440	  (comma-separated / range list, duplicates preserved for
441	  oversubscription) and `pin_current(Option<usize>)` (pin the
442	  calling thread; no-op on `None`). 7 unit tests for the parser
443	  (plain list, range, mixed, duplicates, empty string, reverse
444	  range errors, garbage errors).
445	- `src/perf/mod.rs`: `pub mod pin`.
446	- `src/runtime.rs`: `MultiThreadRuntime::run` gains a
447	  `pin_cores: &[usize]` parameter; actor `i` pins to
448	  `pin_cores[i % pin_cores.len()]` inside its spawned thread
449	  (before the actor factory runs or the probe is created). Empty
450	  slice = unpinned (existing behavior). The lone mt_test updated
451	  to pass `&[]`.
452	- `src/bin/goal1.rs`: `--pin <CORES>` flag (`Option<String>`;
453	  parsed via `pin::parse_cores` in `main`). Uses the first core
454	  of the list (goal1 is single-threaded); extra cores silently
455	  ignored. Pins the main thread before calibration and dispatch.
456	  New `pinning: …` line in the output.
457	- `src/bin/goal2.rs`: `--pin <CORES>` flag, same parsing. Parsed
458	  list passed through to `rt.run(warmup, measurement, &pins)`.
459	  `pinning: actor0→coreN, actor1→coreM, …` line in the output.
460	
461	### A/B on this box (3900X, 12-core / 24-thread)
462	
463	`goal2 0.5 --warmup 0.5` — same workload, three placements:
464	
465	| Pinning     | Throughput | Mean / event | Mean min-p99 | Stdev min-p99 |
466	|---          |---         |---           |---           |---            |
467	| unpinned    | 0.374 M/s  | 632 ns       | 622 ns       | **246 ns**    |
468	| `--pin 0,1` | 0.308 M/s  | 667 ns       | 654 ns       | 171 ns        |
469	| `--pin 0,4` | 0.212 M/s  | 1,496 ns     | 1,487 ns     | **80 ns**     |
470	
471	**Stdev tightens dramatically** with pinning: min-p99 stdev
472	drops 246 → 80 ns (3×) at `--pin 0,4`. Migration noise was a
473	real component of the unpinned variance.
474	
475	**p10–p40 rises** (user's prediction): `--pin 0,4` puts the two
476	actors on different CCX complexes (each CCX = 6 cores on Zen 2),
477	so every ping-pong round-trip incurs a cross-CCX cache coherence
478	transfer. Mean climbs from 632 ns → 1,496 ns — the placement cost.
479	`--pin 0,1` (same CCX) stays close to unpinned throughput
480	(~0.31 M/s vs 0.37) but still narrows stdev somewhat.
481	
482	The bot thinks these numbers are the intended story: pinning
483	trades a potentially higher mean for much more reproducible
484	measurements, which is the right tradeoff for code-change A/B
485	work. The placement itself then becomes a tunable — users pick
486	cores that match what they want to measure (best-case
487	same-complex, worst-case cross-socket, etc.).
488	
489	### `goal1 --pin`
490	
491	Single-thread goal1 sees little throughput change with
492	`--pin 5` (~206 M msg/s, identical to unpinned) — all the work
493	is on one thread anyway, and migration cost is already amortized
494	over the 200 M msg/s inner loop. Value of pinning is purely
495	reproducibility.
496	
497	## Stage 1 complete (0.1.0)
498	
499	Final release of the Stage 1 PoC. Drops the `-N` pre-release
500	suffix and marks the design.md "Stage 1 runtime" goal as shipped.
501	No behavior changes vs 0.1.0-8; this is the "done" marker.
502	
503	- `Cargo.toml`: `0.1.0-8` → `0.1.0`.
504	- `notes/todo.md`: "Stage 1 runtime PoC" moved from
505	  `## In Progress` to `## Done`; reference `[2]` updated to point
506	  at this chores section.
507	- `README.md`: new `## Usage`, `## Reading the band table`, and
508	  `## The bot's understanding` sections inserted after
509	  `## Cloning` and before `## jj Tips for Git Users`. Existing
510	  dual-repo / jj / cross-repo-linking / contributing / license
511	  sections unchanged.
512	- `notes/chores-01.md`: this section.
513	
514	### The Stage 1 ladder at a glance
515	
516	| Version | Landmark |
517	|---|---|
518	| 0.1.0-0 | Cargo bootstrap + plan marker |
519	| 0.1.0-1 | Goal1: single-thread ping-pong runtime |
520	| 0.1.0-3 | `tprobe2` vendored from iiac-perf + inner batching + warmup + clap (folded in 0.1.0-2 retroactively) |
521	| 0.1.0-4 | Always render all 12 band rows |
522	| 0.1.0-5 | CLI polish: `max_term_width = 80`, version banner |
523	| 0.1.0-6 | Apparatus-overhead calibration (two-point fit) |
524	| 0.1.0-7 | Goal2: per-actor threads + `std::sync::mpsc` |
525	| 0.1.0-8 | `--pin` for thread CPU affinity |
526	| **0.1.0** | **Release marker** |
527	
528	### What ships in 0.1.0
529	
530	- Two runtimes: `SingleThreadRuntime` (Goal1) and
531	  `MultiThreadRuntime` (Goal2).
532	- Minimal `Actor` / `Context` / `Message` surface in `src/lib.rs`.
533	- Vendored perf probe stack under `src/perf/` (`tprobe2`,
534	  `band_table`, `ticks`, `fmt`, `overhead`, `pin`), diverged
535	  from `../iiac-perf/src` per actor-x1 conventions but otherwise
536	  tracking upstream.
537	- Two binaries: `goal1` and `goal2`, sharing a common CLI shape.
538	- 22 unit tests; no integration tests yet.
539	
540	### What's next
541	
542	Stage 2 (see [design.md](design.md)): three actors, a richer
543	`Message` with `src_id` / `dst_id` / `send_count`, per-actor
544	constructors and names. Promoting the vendored perf stack to a
545	shared library crate is also on the radar once Stage 2 exposes
546	what's stable.
547

# Chores-01

Discussions and notes on various chores in github compatible markdown.
There is also a [todo.md](todo.md) file and it tracks tasks and in
general there should be a chore section for each task with the why
and how this task will be completed.

See [Chores format](README.md#chores-format)

## Stage 1 runtime — plan marker (0.1.0-0)

Begins the Stage 1 implementation described in [design.md](design.md).
Multi-step ladder (see [Versioning during development](../CLAUDE.md#versioning)):

- `0.1.0-0` — bootstrap Cargo crate + this plan marker (no behavior).
- `0.1.0-1` — Goal1: two actors, single thread, ping-pong an empty
  `Message` for a caller-supplied duration in seconds. Reports
  total messages handled.
- `0.1.0-2` — vendor `tprobe2` (and its transitive deps: `band_table`,
  `ticks`, `fmt_commas*`) from `../iiac-perf/src` into `src/perf/`.
  Wrap per-dispatch `handle_message` calls with `probe.start`/`probe.end`
  using `site_id = actor_id`; print a band-table report at shutdown
  (ns by default, `-t/--ticks` for raw ticks). The bot thinks promoting
  this to a shared crate is worthwhile once Stage 2 is underway, but
  copying in now avoids a cross-repo dependency for the PoC.
- `0.1.0-3` — Goal2: same workload as Goal1 but each actor on its own
  thread, communicating via `std::sync::mpsc` channels. Shutdown by
  dropping senders. Probe instrumented the same way (per-thread
  `TProbe2`, report on join).
- `0.1.0` — final: drop the `-N` suffix, update `notes/todo.md` and
  `notes/README.md`.

### Design decisions recorded here

- **Actor trait signature** deviates from the design sketch by adding
  `&mut self` and a `&mut dyn Context` parameter, where `Context::send(dst_id, msg)`
  is the only way for a handler to emit messages. Required because
  the empty `Message {}` carries no reply-to information and the
  sketched signature `fn handle_message(msg: Message)` has nowhere
  to thread outbound sends through.
- **Two `Context` implementations**: the single-threaded runtime's
  context writes to a `VecDeque`; the multi-threaded runtime's
  context holds `Sender<Message>` per destination. Actors see
  only `&mut dyn Context` and are agnostic to which runtime is
  driving them.
- **x86_64-only for now**: `tprobe2`'s tick counter uses `rdtsc`
  and `iiac-perf/src/ticks.rs` emits a `compile_error!` on other
  arches. Development target is x86_64 so this is acceptable; the
  restriction is documented at the vendored module.

## Goal1: single-thread ping-pong runtime (0.1.0-1)

Implements Goal1 of Stage 1: two actors, one thread, ping-pong an
empty [`Message`] for a caller-supplied duration in seconds, then
report the message count and throughput.

- `src/lib.rs`: `Message` unit struct, `Actor` trait
  (`handle_message(&mut self, &mut dyn Context, Message)`),
  `Context` trait (`send(dst_id: u32, msg: Message)`), and
  `pub mod runtime`.
- `src/runtime.rs`: `SingleThreadRuntime` owns a
  `Vec<Box<dyn Actor>>` and a `VecDeque<(u32, Message)>`; `run_for`
  pops a message, field-split-borrows `actors` and `queue`,
  constructs a private `SingleCtx` wrapping the queue, dispatches,
  counts. Terminates on deadline or queue drain. Unit tests cover
  seed drain, bounded ping-pong count (11 = seed + 5·2), and
  sequential id assignment.
- `src/bin/goal1.rs`: CLI takes one positional `<duration_secs>`
  f64; constructs two `PingPongActor`s (each replies once per
  received message to its peer), seeds one message into actor 0,
  runs, prints `goal1: <count> messages in <secs>s (<M msg/s>)`.

Smoke run on this box: 0.5 s → ~19.3 M messages → ~38.7 M msg/s.

### Deviation from the design sketch

`struct Message {}` in `design.md` is realised as a unit struct
`pub struct Message;` — equivalent externally, instantiates as
`Message` rather than `Message {}`. Matches clippy's preference for
empty types; trivial to convert back if literal match matters.

## Vendored perf probe + Goal1 instrumentation (0.1.0-2)

Copies `tprobe2` and its transitive dependencies into the crate and
wraps every `handle_message` dispatch with a probe scope so Goal1
reports per-dispatch latency alongside message count.

- `Cargo.toml`: version bump to 0.1.0-2; add `hdrhistogram = "7"` and
  `minstant = "0.1"` dependencies.
- `src/perf/mod.rs`: new module root; documents vendor source path
  (`../iiac-perf/src`) and divergences (import paths, `// OK: …`
  on `unwrap*`, one diagnostic string).
- `src/perf/tprobe2.rs`: vendored from `iiac-perf/src/tprobe2.rs`.
  Imports rewritten to `crate::perf::{band_table, ticks}`; two
  `unwrap` calls gained `// OK: …` justifications.
- `src/perf/band_table.rs`: vendored from `iiac-perf/src/band_table.rs`.
  Imports rewritten to `crate::perf::fmt` and `crate::perf::ticks`;
  `unwrap_or(0)` calls gained `// OK: …` justifications.
- `src/perf/ticks.rs`: vendored unchanged except for the
  `compile_error!` diagnostic now mentions `actor-x1 probes`.
- `src/perf/ticks/x86_64.rs`: vendored unchanged.
- `src/perf/fmt.rs`: extracted `fmt_commas` and `fmt_commas_f64`
  from `iiac-perf/src/harness.rs` (lines 160–188) — avoids vendoring
  the 393-line harness for two helpers. `unwrap_or(0)` gained a
  `// OK: …` justification.
- `src/lib.rs`: adds `pub mod perf;`.
- `src/runtime.rs`: `SingleThreadRuntime::new(probe_name)` now takes
  a name, embeds a `TProbe2`, and wraps each `handle_message` call
  with `probe.start(dst_id as u64)` / `probe.end(id)`. `Default` impl
  dropped (new takes an arg). `probe_mut()` exposes the probe for
  end-of-run reporting. Unit tests pass a probe name.
- `src/bin/goal1.rs`: new arg parser supports `-t` / `--ticks`
  (raw-ticks display); runtime constructed as `"goal1.dispatch"`;
  calls `rt.probe_mut().report(as_ticks)` after the dispatch loop.
  Usage errors now `exit(2)` with a usage line instead of panicking.

### Smoke measurements (release build)

- `goal1 0.5` — 10.56 M messages in 0.5s (~21.1 M msg/s).
  Per-dispatch: mean 9 ns, mean min-p99 9 ns, p99 max 67.5 µs
  (likely an OS interrupt in the tail).
- `goal1 0.5 -t` — 10.57 M messages in 0.5s (~21.1 M msg/s).
  Per-dispatch: mean 35 tk, mean min-p99 34 tk, p99 max 114,687 tk.

Adding the probe cuts throughput roughly in half vs. 0.1.0-1
(~38.7 → ~21.1 M msg/s). The bot thinks the delta is dominated by
two `rdtsc` reads plus `Vec::push` per dispatch (~20 ns of the
~47 ns per-dispatch cost); the 9 ns measured by the probe is the
`handle_message` body alone.

## Inner batching, warmup, clap (0.1.0-3)

Adds CLI polish and measurement-quality improvements to Goal1.
Scope:

- **Inner batching** — one probe scope now covers `--inner N`
  consecutive dispatches (default 1). At larger `N` the probe's
  two-`rdtsc`-plus-push apparatus cost is amortized, and stored
  tick values land in a range where the histogram's 0.1 %-relative
  buckets actually resolve jitter.
- **Warmup** — `--warmup SECS` (default **10.0**) runs the same
  dispatch loop with the probe active, then clears probe state;
  measurement phase inherits stable-state CPU freq / cache /
  branch-predictor conditions. Warmup message counts are never
  reported.
- **clap** — hand-rolled arg parsing replaced with a derive-based
  `Cli`. Validates non-negative finite `duration_s` and `warmup`;
  enforces `inner ≥ 1`. Auto-generated `--help` / `--version`.
- **TProbe2 additions** (vendored-code divergence from iiac-perf):
  - `Record` gains a `batch: u64` field.
  - `TProbe2::end_batch(id, n)` appends with `batch = n`; existing
    `end(id)` sets `batch = 1`.
  - `TProbe2::report` now divides stored deltas by `batch` at
    drain time so the histogram always holds per-event cost,
    regardless of scope granularity.
  - `TProbe2::clear()` resets records buffer and histogram;
    called between warmup and measurement.

File-level edits:

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.1.0-3; add
  `clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive", "wrap_help"] }`.
- `src/perf/tprobe2.rs`: `Record.batch`, `end_batch`, `clear`,
  per-event division in `report`. Doc comment at file head lists
  divergences from upstream. Three new tests
  (`end_batch_stores_batch_size`, `report_divides_batched_delta`,
  `clear_empties_records_and_hist`).
- `src/runtime.rs`: `run_for(duration, inner)` wraps each batch
  of `inner` dispatches in a probe scope; site_id is the first
  `dst_id` in the batch. Partial trailing batches (queue empties
  mid-scope) are discarded — scope dropped, count not incremented
  — so throughput numbers reflect whole-batch work only. New test
  `partial_trailing_batch_is_dropped` covers the drop path.
- `src/bin/goal1.rs`: clap-derive `Cli` with `duration_s`,
  `--warmup`, `--inner`, `-t/--ticks`. Runs warmup, clears probe,
  runs measurement, prints count/throughput (now also reports
  `inner=N`), then calls `probe.report`.

### Smoke measurements (release, `--warmup 0`)

| Args | Throughput | Probe mean (min-p99) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `0.5` (inner=1) | 19.7 M msg/s | 9 ns | Apparatus overhead dominates, distribution stuck in two spike buckets |
| `0.5 --inner 1000` | 205 M msg/s | 5 ns | ~10× throughput; per-dispatch mean drops because apparatus is amortized |
| `0.5 --inner 1000 -t` | 204 M msg/s | 18 tk | Ticks view; tail p99-max 449 tk vs ~115 k tk at inner=1 (batch smooths OS interrupts) |

Warmup wall-clock sanity: `goal1 0.3 --warmup 1 --inner 1000` ran
in 1.33s total. Argv error paths (`-1`, `--inner 0`, `--warmup nan`)
all exit 2 with clap-formatted messages.

The bot thinks the inner=1 vs inner=1000 gap (9 ns vs 5 ns
per-event) is ~4 ns of apparatus overhead; 0.1.0-5's overhead
calibration will confirm that estimate directly.

## Always render all 12 percentile bands (0.1.0-4)

Previously the renderer skipped any band where no hdrhistogram
bucket was assigned by mid-rank (`if band_count[i] == 0 { continue;
}`), so tight distributions produced reports with only 3–5 visible
rows and it was hard to tell at a glance whether a band was
genuinely empty or just not rendered. This step always emits all
12 rows (`min-p1` through `p99-max`); empty bands render as zeros
in every numeric column.

File-level edits:

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.1.0-4.
- `src/perf/band_table.rs`: drop the skip-on-empty guard; the band
  loop now computes `(first_v, last_v, range_v, mean_v)` from
  either the band aggregates (for non-empty bands) or `(0, 0, 0, 0)`
  for empty ones. `unwrap_or(0)` `// OK: …` justifications updated
  ("rows always contains n_bands=12 entries").

### Display example (inner=10, 0.5 s)

All 12 rows always visible — the zero-valued bands make the
distribution's shape explicit:

```
    min-p1        4 ns        4 ns        0 ns        8,712     4 ns
    p1-p10        0 ns        0 ns        0 ns            0     0 ns
    ...
    p40-p50       5 ns        5 ns        0 ns    4,794,952     5 ns
    ...
    p99-max       7 ns    3,197 ns    3,190 ns       61,924    10 ns
```

### Caveat — this only fixes visibility, not the bucket-into-band
mapping

The renderer still walks hdrhistogram buckets and assigns each
bucket to exactly one band by cumulative mid-rank. In the inner=10
smoke above, 4,794,952 samples (~89 %) land in `p40-p50` while
`p1-p10` through `p30-p40` stay at zero — the single hot bucket at
"5 ns" got attributed to one band, not spread across the percentile
range its samples actually span. The bot thinks the principled
fix (switch to `hist.value_at_quantile(q)` per band boundary) is
worth considering if the zero-band display starts feeling
misleading; deferred for now.

### Divergence from upstream

Further modification of the vendored `src/perf/band_table.rs`.
Noted in the perf module's divergence list; already has other
actor-x1-specific edits (`// OK:` comments, import paths).

## CLI polish: help width + version banner (0.1.0-5)

Two small quality-of-life tweaks to the `goal1` CLI.

### Help width cap at 80 cols

clap's default help-width uses the terminal width, which on wide
terminals produces hard-to-scan long lines for the `--warmup` and
`--inner` descriptions. `max_term_width = 80` caps rendering at
80 columns while still using terminal width if smaller.

### Version banner

First line of `goal1` output — and of `goal1 -h` — is now
`{CARGO_PKG_NAME} {CARGO_PKG_VERSION}` (e.g. `actor-x1 0.1.0-5`),
matching what `--version` already prints. Helps identify which
build produced a given result sheet (or which help text a user is
reading) when lines are pasted out of context. The existing
`goal1: …` line below carries the binary identity, so the banner
doesn't repeat "goal1". Implemented in two places: a `println!`
at the top of `main` for normal runs, and clap's `before_help`
attribute for help output (`concat!(env!(CARGO_PKG_NAME), " ",
env!(CARGO_PKG_VERSION))` — compile-time concat of package env
vars).

### File-level edits

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.1.0-5.
- `src/bin/goal1.rs`: extend the `Cli` struct's `#[command(...)]`
  attribute with `max_term_width = 80` and
  `before_help = concat!(env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"), " ", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"))`;
  `println!` the same banner at the top of `main` for
  non-help runs.

## Apparatus overhead calibration (0.1.0-6)

Adds a calibration phase between warmup and measurement that fits
the probe's framing cost (the bias on every stored
`end_tsc − start_tsc` delta) and subtracts the appropriate per-
event correction at report time. `--raw` opts out.

### Why two-point (not single-point)

`rdtsc` isn't a serializing instruction, so two back-to-back reads
with nothing between can execute out-of-order and measure ~0 ticks
— below the true apparatus cost. `_mm_lfence` bracketing drains
the pipeline harder than real work does (measured 38 tk on this
box, 2× the real bias). The two-point fit gives the right answer
by varying `inner` on an empty bench and solving
`raw_delta = framing + inner · loop_per_iter` in `f64` (no
integer-division loss at small values).

On this box the fit lands on **framing = 21 tk (~5.5 ns)**,
reproducible across runs. With the correction applied,
`inner=1` probe mean drops from 9 ns (raw) to 4 ns, which matches
the `inner=1000` raw mean of 5 ns (where the correction is
negligible) — both phases converge on the same actual
`handle_message` cost.

### File-level edits

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.1.0-6.
- `src/perf/overhead.rs`: new module. Constants `CAL_WARMUP = 10_000`,
  `CAL_SAMPLES = 1_000`, `N_LOW = 100`, `N_HIGH = 10_000`. `Overhead`
  struct carries `framing_ticks`, `loop_per_iter_ticks`,
  `cal_raw_low_ticks`, `cal_raw_high_ticks`, `cal_duration`.
  `calibrate()` runs the fit; `Overhead::per_event_ticks(batch)`
  returns `framing_ticks / batch.max(1)` for subtraction. Two unit
  tests (amortization math + calibration smoke).
- `src/perf/mod.rs`: `pub mod overhead` + re-export
  `Overhead`/`calibrate`.
- `src/perf/tprobe2.rs`: `TProbe2::report` signature gains
  `overhead: Option<&Overhead>`; drain loop subtracts
  `ovh.per_event_ticks(batch)` from each `per_event_raw` before
  histogram insertion (via `saturating_sub`, clamp at 1). New
  test `report_subtracts_overhead`. Old tests updated to pass `None`.
- `src/bin/goal1.rs`: new `--raw` flag; calibrates on the warmed
  system between `probe.clear()` and the measurement `run_for`;
  prints an `apparatus: …` diagnostic line before the band-table
  report.

### What's *not* subtracted

Only `framing_ticks`. `loop_per_iter_ticks` (from the black_box
empty bench) is kept on `Overhead` for diagnostic visibility but
deliberately not subtracted — the real dispatch loop's per-iter
cost (queue pop, deadline check, SingleCtx construction, dynamic
dispatch) is part of what the user wants to measure, and
`black_box(1)`'s loop_per_iter doesn't model it anyway. User
already signed off on this scope.

### Smoke

- `goal1 0.5 --warmup 0 --inner 1` — 19.9 M msg/s, apparatus
  21 tk / 5.54 ns, probe mean min-p99 **4 ns** (from 9 ns raw).
- `goal1 0.5 --warmup 0 --inner 1000` — 204 M msg/s, apparatus
  correction 0 tk (rounds down), probe mean min-p99 5 ns
  (unchanged — raw already clean at this batch).
- `goal1 0.5 --warmup 0 --inner 1 --raw` — apparatus: "raw (no
  overhead subtraction)"; no calibration run.
- 14/14 tests pass. Calibration adds ~10 ms to default runs.

## Goal2: per-actor threads + mpsc channels (0.1.0-7)

Implements Goal2 of Stage 1: two actors running on their own
threads, ping-ponging an empty [`Message`] over `std::sync::mpsc`
channels, with the same warmup/measurement/calibration lifecycle
as goal1.

### Design decisions

- **Factory closures instead of pre-boxed actors.** `add_actor`
  takes `FnOnce() -> A` where `A: Actor + Send + 'static`; the
  factory runs inside the actor's own thread. Keeps the [`Actor`]
  trait free of a `Send` bound and matches how real actor systems
  typically spawn state.
- **In-band shutdown via internal `Signal` enum.** Channel payload
  is `Signal { User(Message), ClearProbe, Shutdown }`, not
  `Message` directly. Main thread injects `ClearProbe` at the
  warmup → measurement boundary and `Shutdown` after measurement;
  actors match on the variant in their `recv()` loop. Avoids an
  out-of-band atomic flag and the associated polling.
- **`inner` fixed at 1.** Ping-pong keeps at most one message in
  flight per channel, so batched probe scopes (`inner > 1`) could
  never fill — every scope would hit the partial-batch drop path.
  The `--inner` flag from goal1 is not exposed; the hardcoded
  `inner=1` is reflected in the throughput line.
- **Per-actor probes, merged reporting.** Each actor thread owns
  a [`TProbe2`] named `"goal2.dispatch.actor{id}"`. Main collects
  `(count, probe)` on join and prints a per-actor band table (not
  merged — two independent distributions are more informative
  than one blended histogram in a ping-pong workload where the
  actors should be near-identical).
- **Calibration pre-spawn on main thread.** Matches goal1's
  calibration code path. Same CPU → same `Overhead` applies to
  all actor threads. Could be refined later to calibrate
  per-thread, but unnecessary for Stage 1.

### File-level edits

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.1.0-7.
- `src/runtime.rs`: add `MultiThreadRuntime`, `Signal` enum,
  `ActorFactory` alias, `MultiCtx`, and `actor_loop` helper below
  the existing single-thread code. New `#[cfg(test)] mod mt_tests`
  with a ping-pong smoke test (50 ms warmup + 50 ms measurement).
- `src/bin/goal2.rs`: new binary mirroring goal1's CLI shape
  (version banner, help width cap, --warmup, -t/--ticks, --raw),
  no --inner. Constructs `MultiThreadRuntime`, adds two
  `PingPongActor` factories, seeds one message, calls
  `rt.run(warmup, measurement)`, prints summary + apparatus +
  per-actor reports.

### Smoke (release, `--warmup 0`)

- `goal2 0.5` — 116,495 messages in 0.5 s (~0.23 M msg/s).
  Mean per-dispatch ~1.17 µs; median ~1.34 µs; p99 tail to
  ~22 µs (mostly scheduling jitter). Actors near-symmetric
  (58,247 vs 58,248 messages).
- `goal2 -h` — wraps at 80 cols, shows the `actor-x1 0.1.0-7`
  banner, no `--inner` flag.
- `goal2 0.2 --raw` — apparatus: `raw (no overhead subtraction)`;
  calibration skipped.
- goal1 unchanged: still ~200 M msg/s at `--inner 1000`.
- Tests: 15/15 pass (adds `multi_thread_runs_measures_and_shuts_down_cleanly`).

### The ~250× single-thread gap

Goal1 at `inner=1000` hits ~205 M msg/s (~5 ns/dispatch);
goal2 at `inner=1` hits ~0.23 M msg/s (~4 µs/round-trip, ~1.2 µs
per-event inside the probe scope). The bot thinks the gap is
dominated by: (1) mpsc's `Mutex` + condvar wake for every
message, (2) two cross-thread context switches per ping-pong
round, (3) cache-line bouncing between the two actors' threads.
An unbounded channel or a lock-free mpsc implementation would
close some of this but the context-switch floor is fundamental.

## `--pin` for thread CPU affinity (0.1.0-8)

Adds `--pin <CORES>` to both binaries. Tightens the stdev of
probe reports by eliminating OS thread-migration noise, at the
cost of exposing placement sensitivity in goal2 (pinning to
cross-CCX cores raises the mean because every message forces a
cross-complex cache coherence round-trip).

Adapted from `../iiac-perf/src/pin.rs` with only the two
primitives we need (`parse_cores`, `pin_current`); the affinity
save/restore, `--no-pin-cal`, and mask-summary helpers are
elided — Stage 1 doesn't need them.

### File-level edits

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.1.0-8; add `core_affinity = "0.8"`.
- `src/perf/pin.rs`: new module. `parse_cores(&str) -> Result<Vec<usize>, String>`
  (comma-separated / range list, duplicates preserved for
  oversubscription) and `pin_current(Option<usize>)` (pin the
  calling thread; no-op on `None`). 7 unit tests for the parser
  (plain list, range, mixed, duplicates, empty string, reverse
  range errors, garbage errors).
- `src/perf/mod.rs`: `pub mod pin`.
- `src/runtime.rs`: `MultiThreadRuntime::run` gains a
  `pin_cores: &[usize]` parameter; actor `i` pins to
  `pin_cores[i % pin_cores.len()]` inside its spawned thread
  (before the actor factory runs or the probe is created). Empty
  slice = unpinned (existing behavior). The lone mt_test updated
  to pass `&[]`.
- `src/bin/goal1.rs`: `--pin <CORES>` flag (`Option<String>`;
  parsed via `pin::parse_cores` in `main`). Uses the first core
  of the list (goal1 is single-threaded); extra cores silently
  ignored. Pins the main thread before calibration and dispatch.
  New `pinning: …` line in the output.
- `src/bin/goal2.rs`: `--pin <CORES>` flag, same parsing. Parsed
  list passed through to `rt.run(warmup, measurement, &pins)`.
  `pinning: actor0→coreN, actor1→coreM, …` line in the output.

### A/B on this box (3900X, 12-core / 24-thread)

`goal2 0.5 --warmup 0.5` — same workload, three placements:

| Pinning     | Throughput | Mean / event | Mean min-p99 | Stdev min-p99 |
|---          |---         |---           |---           |---            |
| unpinned    | 0.374 M/s  | 632 ns       | 622 ns       | **246 ns**    |
| `--pin 0,1` | 0.308 M/s  | 667 ns       | 654 ns       | 171 ns        |
| `--pin 0,4` | 0.212 M/s  | 1,496 ns     | 1,487 ns     | **80 ns**     |

**Stdev tightens dramatically** with pinning: min-p99 stdev
drops 246 → 80 ns (3×) at `--pin 0,4`. Migration noise was a
real component of the unpinned variance.

**p10–p40 rises** (user's prediction): `--pin 0,4` puts the two
actors on different CCX complexes (each CCX = 6 cores on Zen 2),
so every ping-pong round-trip incurs a cross-CCX cache coherence
transfer. Mean climbs from 632 ns → 1,496 ns — the placement cost.
`--pin 0,1` (same CCX) stays close to unpinned throughput
(~0.31 M/s vs 0.37) but still narrows stdev somewhat.

The bot thinks these numbers are the intended story: pinning
trades a potentially higher mean for much more reproducible
measurements, which is the right tradeoff for code-change A/B
work. The placement itself then becomes a tunable — users pick
cores that match what they want to measure (best-case
same-complex, worst-case cross-socket, etc.).

### `goal1 --pin`

Single-thread goal1 sees little throughput change with
`--pin 5` (~206 M msg/s, identical to unpinned) — all the work
is on one thread anyway, and migration cost is already amortized
over the 200 M msg/s inner loop. Value of pinning is purely
reproducibility.

## Stage 1 complete (0.1.0)

Final release of the Stage 1 PoC. Drops the `-N` pre-release
suffix and marks the design.md "Stage 1 runtime" goal as shipped.
No behavior changes vs 0.1.0-8; this is the "done" marker.

- `Cargo.toml`: `0.1.0-8` → `0.1.0`.
- `notes/todo.md`: "Stage 1 runtime PoC" moved from
  `## In Progress` to `## Done`; reference `[2]` updated to point
  at this chores section.
- `README.md`: new `## Usage`, `## Reading the band table`, and
  `## The bot's understanding` sections inserted after
  `## Cloning` and before `## jj Tips for Git Users`. Existing
  dual-repo / jj / cross-repo-linking / contributing / license
  sections unchanged.
- `notes/chores-01.md`: this section.

### The Stage 1 ladder at a glance

| Version | Landmark |
|---|---|
| 0.1.0-0 | Cargo bootstrap + plan marker |
| 0.1.0-1 | Goal1: single-thread ping-pong runtime |
| 0.1.0-3 | `tprobe2` vendored from iiac-perf + inner batching + warmup + clap (folded in 0.1.0-2 retroactively) |
| 0.1.0-4 | Always render all 12 band rows |
| 0.1.0-5 | CLI polish: `max_term_width = 80`, version banner |
| 0.1.0-6 | Apparatus-overhead calibration (two-point fit) |
| 0.1.0-7 | Goal2: per-actor threads + `std::sync::mpsc` |
| 0.1.0-8 | `--pin` for thread CPU affinity |
| **0.1.0** | **Release marker** |

### What ships in 0.1.0

- Two runtimes: `SingleThreadRuntime` (Goal1) and
  `MultiThreadRuntime` (Goal2).
- Minimal `Actor` / `Context` / `Message` surface in `src/lib.rs`.
- Vendored perf probe stack under `src/perf/` (`tprobe2`,
  `band_table`, `ticks`, `fmt`, `overhead`, `pin`), diverged
  from `../iiac-perf/src` per actor-x1 conventions but otherwise
  tracking upstream.
- Two binaries: `goal1` and `goal2`, sharing a common CLI shape.
- 22 unit tests; no integration tests yet.

### What's next

Stage 2 (see [design.md](design.md)): three actors, a richer
`Message` with `src_id` / `dst_id` / `send_count`, per-actor
constructors and names. Promoting the vendored perf stack to a
shared library crate is also on the radar once Stage 2 exposes
what's stable.

content.text:
I'm acquainted. Quick orientation:

**Project**: `actor-x1` — Rust experiment in the CSP / actor model. Currently at **v0.1.0 (Stage 1 complete)** on clean working copy, `@` is an empty working commit on top of the 0.1.0 release marker.

**Stage 1 shipped** (per `notes/chores-01.md`):
- `SingleThreadRuntime` (Goal1) — two actors ping-ponging on one thread, ~200M msg/s at `--inner 1000`.
- `MultiThreadRuntime` (Goal2) — per-actor threads over `std::sync::mpsc`, ~0.23–0.37M msg/s.
- Vendored perf probe stack under `src/perf/` (`tprobe2`, `band_table`, `ticks`, `fmt`, `overhead`, `pin`) copied from `../iiac-perf/src`, diverged with actor-x1 conventions (`// OK:` justifications, import paths).
- Two bins (`goal1`, `goal2`) with warmup / calibration / `--pin` / `--ticks` / `--raw` / `--inner` (goal1 only). 22 unit tests.

**Structure**:
- `src/lib.rs` (41 lines): `Message`, `Actor`, `Context` traits
- `src/runtime.rs` (460 lines): both runtimes
- `src/bin/{goal1,goal2}.rs`: the two binaries
- `src/perf/*`: vendored probe stack

**What's next per design.md**: Stage 2 — three actors A/B/C with a richer `Message { src_id, dst_id, send_count }`, per-actor `new(id, name)` + `name()`.

**Process reminders picked up from CLAUDE.md**:
- Dual-repo (`.` app + `.claude` session), both `jj`
- Two-checkpoint commit flow with explicit user approval; never push/finalize without it
- After finalize → hard stop, wait for explicit direction
- Every plan starts with a version bump; `-0/-1/-…` ladder for multi-step
- Doc comments on every `.rs` file / fn / method; `// OK: …` on every `unwrap*` in non-test code
- Speculation prefixed `"The bot thinks …"`

Ready for direction. What are we working on?

message.content: I want to extract "perf" into tprobe a tprobe module, thoughts

content.text:
Three flavors to pick between — main tradeoff is how much boundary you want:

1. **In-tree rename** (`src/perf/` → `src/tprobe/`): trivial, one commit, pure namespace change. Almost no benefit over status quo.
2. **Workspace crate** inside this repo (`tprobe/` as a workspace member, `actor-x1` depends on it by path): forces a clean `pub` surface, reusable across future bins/tests, still one repo so refactors are atomic. The bot thinks this is the right next step — Stage 2 is the trigger the 0.1.0-2 chores note called out, and all the divergence from `iiac-perf` has settled.
3. **External sibling crate** (`../tprobe/`): premature — API isn't stable (Stage 2 may rework `Overhead` or add per-thread calibration), and cross-repo coupling is exactly what we avoided by vendoring in the first place.

Recommendation: (2), workspace split, as a multi-step plan (`0.2.0-0` marker → move files → rewire `actor-x1` to depend on path → final `0.2.0`). Want me to draft that plan, or did you mean one of the other two?

Before I draft the ladder, two decisions to lock in:

**Q1 — Layout.** Two options:
- **(a) Mixed root**: keep `Cargo.toml` as `[package]` for `actor-x1` AND add `[workspace]`. `tprobe/` is a subdir member. `actor-x1`'s source stays in `src/`. Minimal churn.
- **(b) Virtual root**: convert root to `[workspace]`-only with `crates/actor-x1/` and `crates/tprobe/`. Cleaner symmetric layout, but moves every `actor-x1` file.

Recommendation: **(a)**. The extraction is about splitting `perf/` out cleanly; moving all of `actor-x1`'s files at the same time is unrelated churn and bigger blast radius.

**Q2 — Naming.** The crate is `tprobe`, but the star type is `TProbe2` and the file is `tprobe2.rs`. Keep the "2" (historical, matches upstream `iiac-perf`), or drop it inside the new crate (so the crate root re-exports `TProbe`)?

Recommendation: **drop the "2"**. Inside the new crate, `TProbe2` → `TProbe`, `tprobe2.rs` → `tprobe.rs`. The "2" only meant "second try" inside `iiac-perf`; in its own crate it's just noise. Trivially re-added if you disagree. (Divergence from upstream is already substantial — one more isn't costly.)

**Draft ladder** (assuming (a) + drop "2", multi-step):

| Version | Landmark |
|---|---|
| `0.2.0-0` | Plan marker. Add `[workspace]` to root `Cargo.toml`; create empty `tprobe/` skeleton (`Cargo.toml` + `src/lib.rs` stub). No file moves. |
| `0.2.0-1` | Move `src/perf/*` → `tprobe/src/*`. Rewrite internal imports (`crate::perf::x` → `crate::x`). Move `hdrhistogram`/`minstant`/`core_affinity` deps from root to `tprobe/Cargo.toml`. Add `tprobe = { path = "tprobe" }` to `actor-x1` deps. Rewire call sites in `runtime.rs`, `goal1.rs`, `goal2.rs` (`crate::perf::x` → `tprobe::x`). Drop `pub mod perf;` from `src/lib.rs`. Tests + binaries unchanged in behavior. |
| `0.2.0-2` | Rename: `TProbe2` → `TProbe`, `tprobe2.rs` → `tprobe.rs`. Pure rename; sweep call sites. |
| `0.2.0` | Done marker. Drop suffix, update `notes/todo.md` (add this as Done, maybe a Stage 2 entry), add chores sections, update `README.md` if the source tree diagram or contributor notes reference `src/perf`. |

Answer Q1/Q2 (or override), and I'll flesh out the `0.2.0-0` plan details for approval before anything gets written.

Layout (b) locked in. Q2 still open — do you want the `TProbe2` → `TProbe` / `tprobe2.rs` → `tprobe.rs` rename or keep the "2"?

**Draft ladder for (b)**:

| Version | Landmark |
|---|---|
| `0.2.0-0` | Plan marker. Bump root `Cargo.toml` to `0.2.0-0`; add chore section describing the workspace split. No structural change. |
| `0.2.0-1` | Workspace layout. Move `Cargo.toml` + `src/` → `crates/actor-x1/`. Root `Cargo.toml` becomes virtual workspace (`[workspace] members = ["crates/actor-x1", "crates/tprobe"]`, `resolver = "2"`). Create empty `crates/tprobe/` skeleton (`Cargo.toml` + `src/lib.rs` docstring stub). No code moved yet — perf still inside actor-x1. `cargo build`, `cargo test`, both binaries unchanged. |
| `0.2.0-2` | Move perf into tprobe. Move `crates/actor-x1/src/perf/*` → `crates/tprobe/src/*`. Rewrite internal imports (`crate::perf::x` → `crate::x`). Migrate `hdrhistogram`/`minstant`/`core_affinity` deps from actor-x1 to tprobe's `Cargo.toml`. Add `tprobe = { path = "../tprobe" }` to actor-x1's deps. Drop `pub mod perf;` from actor-x1's `src/lib.rs`. Rewire call sites in `runtime.rs`, `goal1.rs`, `goal2.rs` (`crate::perf::x` → `tprobe::x`). Behavior unchanged; 22 tests pass. |
| `0.2.0-3` | *(Optional, pending Q2)* Rename: `TProbe2` → `TProbe`, `tprobe2.rs` → `tprobe.rs`. Pure sweep. Skip this step if keeping the "2". |
| `0.2.0` | Done marker. Drop suffix. `notes/todo.md`: add as Done, maybe add a Stage 2 entry. `notes/chores-01.md`: add per-step sections. `README.md`: update if the source tree diagram references `src/perf/`. |

**Things I'll need to check during -1** so they don't surprise us:
- `.vc-config.toml` — may reference `src/` paths; verify or update.
- `Cargo.lock` stays at root (shared across workspace — standard).
- `.gitignore` already ignores `target/`; workspace moves `target/` effectively to root which already matches.
- `rust-toolchain.toml`? (none present, so nothing to move.)
- Any `#![cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]` in `ticks/` stays in tprobe; fine.

Answer Q2 and confirm the ladder, and I'll start on `0.2.0-0`.
Ladder confirmed. Starting `0.2.0-0` — plan marker only (version bump + chore section + todo entry).

### What's next

Stage 2 (see [design.md](design.md)): three actors, a richer
`Message` with `src_id` / `dst_id` / `send_count`, per-actor
constructors and names. Promoting the vendored perf stack to a
shared library crate is also on the radar once Stage 2 exposes
what's stable.

## Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0)

Converts the single-crate repo into a Cargo workspace with
`actor-x1` and a new `tprobe` crate carved out of `src/perf/`.
The 0.1.0-2 chores note flagged "promoting this to a shared
crate is worthwhile once Stage 2 is underway" — Stage 1 has
shipped and divergence from upstream `iiac-perf` has settled,
so this is the natural next step before Stage 2 adds new
actors and messages.

Multi-step ladder:

- `0.2.0-0` — Plan marker. Bump root `Cargo.toml` to `0.2.0-0`;
  this chore section; `notes/todo.md` entry under `## In Progress`.
  No structural change.
- `0.2.0-1` — Workspace layout. Move root `Cargo.toml` + `src/`
  into `crates/actor-x1/`. Root `Cargo.toml` becomes a virtual
  workspace (`[workspace]` only, `resolver = "2"`,
  `members = ["crates/actor-x1", "crates/tprobe"]`). Create
  `crates/tprobe/` skeleton (`Cargo.toml` + `src/lib.rs`
  docstring stub). `src/perf/*` still lives inside `actor-x1`;
  no probe file moves yet. `cargo test` passes; both binaries
  behave identically.
- `0.2.0-2` — Move perf into tprobe. Move
  `crates/actor-x1/src/perf/*` → `crates/tprobe/src/*`;
  rewrite internal imports (`crate::perf::x` → `crate::x`).
  Migrate `hdrhistogram`, `minstant`, `core_affinity` deps
  from actor-x1 to tprobe's `Cargo.toml`. Add
  `tprobe = { path = "../tprobe" }` to actor-x1's deps. Drop
  `pub mod perf;` from actor-x1's `src/lib.rs`. Rewire call
  sites in `runtime.rs`, `goal1.rs`, `goal2.rs`
  (`crate::perf::x` → `tprobe::x`). Behavior unchanged; all
  tests pass.
- `0.2.0-3` — Rename `TProbe2` → `TProbe` and
  `tprobe2.rs` → `tprobe.rs`. Pure sweep across the new crate
  and its callers. The "2" only communicated "second version
  of the probe" inside `iiac-perf`; outside that repo it's
  noise. Upstream divergence is already substantial, so the
  rename does not meaningfully raise any future re-sync cost.
- `0.2.0` — Done marker. Drop `-N` suffix. `notes/todo.md`:
  move this task to `## Done`. `notes/chores-01.md`: this
  section. `README.md`: update any source-tree or contributor
  notes that reference `src/perf/`.

### Design decisions recorded here

- **Virtual workspace, `crates/` subdir** (option (b) in the
  plan discussion): root is `[workspace]`-only, both packages
  live under `crates/`. Cleaner symmetric layout than mixing
  `[package]` and `[workspace]` at the root, at the cost of a
  one-time move of every actor-x1 source file.
- **Crate name `tprobe` drops the "2"**. The type rename is
  deferred to its own step (`0.2.0-3`) to keep the move diff
  (`0.2.0-2`) separate from the rename diff — easier review
  for each.
- **Perf deps move with the perf code.** `hdrhistogram`,
  `minstant`, and `core_affinity` migrate out of actor-x1's
  `Cargo.toml` in `0.2.0-2`; actor-x1 picks them up transitively
  via `tprobe` without redeclaring.

1	# CLAUDE.md - Bot Instructions
2	
3	## Project Structure
4	
5	This project uses **two separate jj-git repos**:
6	
7	1. **App repo** (`/` — project root): Contains the application source code.
8	2. **Bot session repo** (`/.claude/`): Contains Claude Code session data.
9	
10	Both repos are managed with `jj` (Jujutsu), which coexists with git.
11	
12	## Repo Paths (relative from project root)
13	
14	- App repo: `.` (project root)
15	- Bot session repo: `.claude`
16	  (symlink from `~/.claude/projects/<path-to-project-root>/.claude`)
17	
18	## Working Directory
19	
20	Prefer staying in the project root. Use `-R` flags or absolute paths
21	to target other directories rather than `cd`. If `cd` seems necessary,
22	discuss with the user first — losing track of cwd causes subtle
23	command failures downstream.
24	
25	## Memory
26	
27	Do not use the bot's per-project memory directory
28	(`~/.claude/projects/<path>/memory/`). In a dual-repo setup with
29	CLAUDE.md it provides no capability CLAUDE.md doesn't already cover,
30	and it loses on discoverability:
31	
32	- **CLAUDE.md** — at the repo root, a well-known location for bot
33	  instructions, committed, reviewable, visible to every collaborator
34	  (human or bot).
35	- **Memory directory** — hidden under the user's home, tied to one
36	  machine, invisible to anyone but the bot, never diffed or reviewed.
37	
38	Easy for everyone to find beats convenient for the bot alone. Put
39	durable context in CLAUDE.md (or committed `notes/`) instead.
40	
41	## Speculation marker
42	
43	Durable text the bot writes — CLAUDE.md, `notes/`, commit bodies,
44	chores sections — should stick to observations and direct descriptions
45	of the code or data. If a mechanism, hypothesis, or causal claim
46	enters the text, prefix it with "The bot thinks ..." so a reader can
47	tell the measured from the inferred.
48	
49	**Why:** unmarked speculation reads like evidence, and a future reader
50	(or the bot on a later session) can pick it up as a known fact when
51	it's not. Measured / inferred is a distinction worth keeping visible
52	in the written record.
53	
54	**How to apply:** observations and factual descriptions need no
55	marker. Prefix with "The bot thinks ..." (or a close variant like
56	"The bot's guess is ...") when the claim is a mechanism ("X wins
57	because Y caches better"), a cause ("the drift was due to thermal
58	state"), a prediction ("this should scale linearly"), or any
59	reasoning not directly supported by the data on hand.
60	
61	## Committing
62	
63	Use `-R` (`--repository`) at the end to target the correct repo. Use
64	relative paths to reduce noise. Putting `-R` last keeps the verb/action
65	visible at the start of the command.
66	
67	### App repo
68	```
69	jj commit -m \
70	"title" \
71	-m "body
72	
73	ochid: /.claude/<changeID>" \
74	-R .
75	```
76	
77	### Bot session repo
78	```
79	jj commit -m \
80	"title" \
81	-m "body
82	
83	ochid: /<changeID>" \
84	-R .claude
85	```
86	
87	## jj Basics
88	
89	- `jj st -R .` / `jj st -R .claude` — show working copy status
90	- `jj log -R .` / `jj log -R .claude` — show commit log
91	- `jj commit -m "title" -m "body" -R <repo>` — finalize working copy into a commit
92	- `jj describe -m "title" -m "body" -R <repo>` — set description without committing
93	- `jj git push --bookmark <name> -R <repo>` — push a bookmark (no
94	  `--allow-new` flag; jj pushes new bookmarks without special flags)
95	- In jj, the working copy (@) is always a mutable commit being edited.
96	  `jj commit` finalizes it and creates a new empty working copy on top.
97	- The `.claude` repo always has uncommitted changes during an active
98	  session because session data updates continuously.
99	
100	## Commit Message Style
101	
102	Use [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) with
103	a version suffix:
104	
105	```
106	<type>: <short description> (<version>)
107	```
108	
109	- **Title**: target ~50 chars, short summary of *what* changed.
110	  Include the version. Common types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`,
111	  `test`, `docs`, `chore`.
112	- **App-repo body**: short intro paragraph (1–3 sentences), then a
113	  terse bullet list. Each bullet corresponds one-to-one with the
114	  edits structure already documented in `notes/chores-*.md` for
115	  this step — just the file and a one-line gist (e.g.
116	  `README.md: new Overview intro`). Do *not* restate the detail
117	  that lives in chores; the commit body is a scan-able index, not
118	  a duplicate. The chores section is the source of truth.
119	- **Session-repo body**: terse intro + a few session-activity
120	  bullets. Doesn't need to mirror chores since it describes
121	  in-session work, not code changes.
122	- Examples:
123	  - `feat: add fix-ochid subcommand (0.22.0)`
124	  - `fix: fix-ochid prefix bug (0.22.1)`
125	  - `refactor: deduplicate common CLI flags (0.21.1)`
126	
127	## Pre-commit Requirements
128	
129	### User approval
130	
131	Never execute commit, squash, push, or finalize commands without the
132	user's explicit approval. Present changes for review first; only run
133	them after the user confirms. This applies to late changes too —
134	pause for review before squashing into an existing commit.
135	
136	### Review before proposing the commit block
137	
138	After finishing a unit of work, **summarize what changed and stop
139	there**. Do not pre-emptively lay out the Checkpoint-1 commit
140	commands. Wait for the user to signal review is complete before
141	proposing the commit block. Changes during review are the norm,
142	not the exception; proposing commit text too early creates noise
143	and signals that I consider the work done when it usually isn't.
144	
145	This applies per-step in a multi-step flow too — each step gets a
146	review pause before its commit block appears.
147	
148	Signals that review is complete include explicit approval ("let's
149	commit", "looks good, commit it") **and any directive to start the
150	next step** ("do step 4", "next", "go N+1"). In that case the
151	previous step must be committed first — always commit the current
152	step before starting the next; don't ask.
153	
154	### Notes references
155	
156	Multiple references must be separated: `[2],[3]` not `[2,3]` or `[2][3]`.
157	See [Todo format](notes/README.md#todo-format) for details.
158	
159	### Versioning
160	
161	Every plan must start with a version bump. Choose the approach based
162	on scope:
163	
164	- **Single-step** (recommended for mechanical/focused changes): bump
165	  directly to `X.Y.Z`, implement in one commit. Simpler history.
166	- **Multi-step** (for exploratory/large changes): bump to `X.Y.Z-0`,
167	  implement across multiple commits incrementing the numeric
168	  suffix. The final commit drops the suffix.
169	
170	The plan should recommend one approach and get user approval before
171	starting.
172	
173	For multi-step:
174	
175	1. Bump version to `X.Y.Z-0` with the plan and commit as a chore
176	   marker.
177	2. Implement in one or more `X.Y.Z-N` commits (increment N as
178	   needed).
179	3. Final commit bumps to `X.Y.Z` (no suffix), updates
180	   `notes/todo.md` and `notes/chores-*.md` — this is the "done"
181	   marker.
182	
183	**Why numeric suffixes (`-0`, `-1`, …) rather than `-devN`:**
184	semver pre-release identifiers may consist of a single numeric
185	component, and they compare numerically per spec. So
186	`X.Y.Z-1 < X.Y.Z-2 < … < X.Y.Z` correctly orders the dev ladder
187	below the done marker. Cargo accepts this form. The `-dev` prefix
188	adds no information the git log doesn't already convey and
189	doubles typing per commit.
190	
191	The final release commit (no suffix) signals completion rather than
192	amending prior commits. This keeps history readable and makes it easy
193	to see which commits were exploratory vs final.
194	
195	### Chores section headers
196	
197	Chores section headers use trailing version format:
198	
199	```
200	## Description (X.Y.Z)
201	```
202	
203	Example: `` ## Add `fn claude-symlink` (0.27.0) ``
204	
205	### Pre-commit checklist
206	
207	Before proposing a commit, run all of the following and fix any issues:
208	
209	1. `cargo fmt`
